root_text.ud6.9:1.2: ekaṁ samayaṁ bhagavā sāvatthiyaṁ viharati jetavane anāthapiṇḍikassa ārāme.
root_text.ud6.9:1.3: Tena kho pana samayena bhagavā rattandhakāratimisāyaṁ abbhokāse nisinno hoti telappadīpesu jhāyamānesu.
root_text.ud6.9:2.1: Tena kho pana samayena sambahulā adhipātakā tesu telappadīpesu āpātaparipātaṁ anayaṁ āpajjanti, byasanaṁ āpajjanti, anayabyasanaṁ āpajjanti.
root_text.ud6.9:2.2: Addasā kho bhagavā te sambahule adhipātake tesu telappadīpesu āpātaparipātaṁ anayaṁ āpajjante, byasanaṁ āpajjante, anayabyasanaṁ āpajjante.
root_text.ud6.9:3.1: Atha kho bhagavā etamatthaṁ viditvā tāyaṁ velāyaṁ imaṁ udānaṁ udānesi:

translation_text.ud6.9:1.2: At one time the Buddha was staying near Sāvatthī in Jeta’s Grove, Anāthapiṇḍika’s monastery.
translation_text.ud6.9:1.3: Now at that time the Buddha was meditating in the open during the dark of night, while oil lamps were burning.
translation_text.ud6.9:2.1: And many moths were falling down and crashing down into the lamps, coming to grief and ruin.
translation_text.ud6.9:2.2: The Buddha saw the moths coming to grief.
translation_text.ud6.9:3.1: Then, understanding this matter, on that occasion the Buddha expressed this heartfelt sentiment:
translation_text.ud6.9:4.3: Like moths falling in the flame,
translation_text.ud6.9:4.4: some have become fixed in what is seen or heard.”

variant_text.ud6.9:2.1: anayabyasanaṁ āpajjanti → etthantare pāṭho si potthake
